title: Mounting A Monster Epic: Frankenstein's Costume, Production Design & Prosthetics Team
text: Production designer Tamara Deverell emphasized del Toro's distinctive approach, noting his desire for us to do something operatic and theatrical. The film incorporates symbolic circular and cylinder motifs with a dominant red color palette throughout. Caravaggio paintings served as visual benchmarks. Deverell explained that Victor's childhood brought through his story is represented through the color red that runs consistently throughout the narrative. The production achieved rare access to the Tiffany archive, bringing luxurious gothic art nouveau aesthetics to the screen. Costume designer Kate Hawley noted that del Toro comes with his own references and images and creates detailed character bibles. The protagonist's wardrobe reflects David Bowie and Prince influences—described as old world and bohemian sensibilities befitting an artistic performer. Jacob Elordi's creature costume featured yellow bands with Christ-like significance. The production created 1,252 background costumes, 53 stunt costumes, and 68 unique outfits for the main cast. Prosthetics designer Mike Hill revealed the extensive application process: 54 prosthetic silicon pieces composed the creature design, requiring 8 people and 10 hours for full-body application. Elordi worked on an alternating schedule with prosthetics applied 50 times throughout filming.
